decisions.title: V1 MVP target date
decisions.detail: The main goal for V1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working MVP by September 1st.

evidence.quote: The main goal for V1 is a working MVP by September 1st.

decisions.title: V1 deadline is a hard constraint
decisions.detail: The September 1st deadline for V1 is a hard constraint due to the marketing campaign launch scheduled for September 5th.

evidence.quote: We have a hard constraint on the deadline because of the marketing campaign launch on Sept 5th.

decisions.title: V1 must include user authentication
decisions.detail: The V1 MVP must have user authentication functionality.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: V1 must include a working project dashboard
decisions.detail: The V1 MVP must include a working project dashboard.

evidence.quote: V1 must have user authentication and a working project dashboard.

decisions.title: System performance requirement
decisions.detail: The system needs to be 'super fast'.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure it's super fast.

decisions.title: Frontend technology decision: React
decisions.detail: React has been decided as the technology for the frontend.

evidence.quote: For the stack, I've decided: we are using React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Backend technology decision: Python (FastAPI)
decisions.detail: Python (FastAPI) has been decided as the technology for the backend service.

evidence.quote: For the stack, I've decided: we are using React for the frontend and Python (FastAPI) for the backend service.

decisions.title: Database technology decision: PostgreSQL
decisions.detail: PostgreSQL will be used as the database, following a standard relational model.

evidence.quote: We'll use PostgreSQL. Standard relational model is fine.

decisions.title: Open question: Session management technology
decisions.detail: A question was raised regarding the choice for session management: Redis or standard database sessions?

evidence.quote: But wait, what about session management? Should we use Redis or just standard database sessions?

decisions.title: Redis deferred to V2
decisions.detail: The use of Redis for session management will be deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker.

evidence.quote: Let's defer Redis to V2 unless it's a performance blocker.

decisions.title: Standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1
decisions.detail: For V1, standard PostgreSQL database sessions will be used for session management to keep it simple and save budget.

evidence.quote: Let's stick to standard postgres sessions for now to save budget.

decisions.title: External authentication provider preference
decisions.detail: The team should use an external authentication provider (e.g., Auth0 or Firebase Auth) rather than building authentication in-house.

evidence.quote: We don't want to build it ourselves, right?

decisions.title: Firebase Authentication for V1
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication will be integrated for user authentication in V1 due to its speed, ease of social logins, and matching pricing requirements.

evidence.quote: Actually, for V1, let's just integrate Firebase Authentication. It's quick, handles social logins easily, and matches our pricing requirements.

decisions.title: Required Firebase Auth methods
decisions.detail: Firebase Authentication must be configured to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

evidence.quote: We will configure it to allow Google sign-in and email logins.

decisions.title: Initial user registration fields
decisions.detail: For user registration, the system will initially collect email, full name, and role. Address or telephone number are not needed for now.

evidence.quote: Just email, full name, and role. We don't need address or telephone number for now.
evidence.approximate_location: Sarah's statement on user registration fields

decisions.title: Company name required for B2B registration
decisions.detail: For B2B purposes, company name is a required field during user signup.

evidence.quote: Wait, we need to collect company name too! We are B2B.

decisions.title: Final user registration fields
decisions.detail: The required fields for user registration are email, full name, role, and company name.

evidence.quote: Email, full name, role, and company name.

decisions.title: Proposed backend deployment platform: Cloud Run
decisions.detail: Cloud Run was proposed for deploying the FastAPI backend application.

evidence.quote: I was thinking Cloud Run for the FastAPI app

decisions.title: Proposed frontend deployment platform: Vercel
decisions.detail: Vercel was proposed for deploying the React frontend application, which was accepted.

evidence.quote: and Vercel for the React app.
Vercel is fine

decisions.title: Database security constraint: Private VPC
decisions.detail: The PostgreSQL database instance must be placed in a private VPC and connected via Serverless VPC Access to prevent exposure to the public internet. This is a strict technical security constraint.

evidence.quote: Yes, we will place the database in a private VPC and connect Cloud Run via Serverless VPC Access. That way the postgres instance is not exposed to the public internet. This is a strict technical constraint for security.
evidence.approximate_location: John's explanation of Cloud Run security

decisions.title: Authentication flow testing requirement
decisions.detail: The authentication flow needs to be tested thoroughly.

evidence.quote: We need to test the auth flow thoroughly.
evidence.approximate_location: Emma's statement on auth flow testing

decisions.title: Token expiration policy
decisions.detail: Session token expiration will initially be set to 24 hours, with the possibility of adjustment later.

evidence.quote: Let's set token expiration to 24 hours.

decisions.title: Dashboard required metrics
decisions.detail: The project dashboard must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

evidence.quote: We need active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

decisions.title: Detail dashboard features in next sync
decisions.detail: Further detailing of dashboard features will be handled in the next synchronization meeting.

evidence.quote: Let's detail the dashboard features in the next sync.

decisions.title: Open question: Firebase provisioning ownership and deadline
decisions.detail: An open question was raised regarding who is responsible for handling Firebase provisioning, with config keys needed by next week.

evidence.quote: One open question: who is handling the Firebase provisioning? I need the config keys by next week.

decisions.title: Firebase provisioning assigned to Mike
decisions.detail: Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning.

evidence.quote: I'll assign that to Mike.

decisions.title: Firebase config keys deadline
decisions.detail: Mike is expected to have the Firebase configuration keys ready by July 8th.

evidence.quote: He should have it ready by July 8th.

decisions.title: Sync with marketing team on dashboard design
decisions.detail: A synchronization meeting with the marketing team on the dashboard design is planned for next Wednesday.

evidence.quote: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.

implementation_scope: Integrate Firebase Authentication supporting Google sign-in and email logins.
Implement user registration with required fields: email, full name, role, and company name.
Implement standard PostgreSQL database sessions for V1 session management.
Set session token expiration policy to 24 hours.
Develop dashboard features to display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Performance optimization to ensure fast system response.
technical_constraints: Database technology: PostgreSQL with a standard relational model.
Authentication must use an external provider; Firebase Authentication for V1.
PostgreSQL database instance must be in a private VPC, connected via Serverless VPC Access, preventing public internet exposure.
Redis for session management is deferred to V2 unless performance dictates otherwise.

acceptance_criteria: Given Firebase Authentication is integrated, When a new user attempts to register, Then they must be able to register using their email and a password.
Given Firebase Authentication is integrated, When a new user attempts to register, Then they must be able to register using Google Sign-in.
Given a user is on the registration form, When they successfully submit the form, Then the system must collect and store their email, full name, role, and company name.
Given a user is on the registration form, When they attempt to submit the form with a missing or invalid email, Then the system must prevent registration and display an appropriate error message.
Given a user is on the registration form, When they attempt to submit the form with a missing full name, Then the system must prevent registration and display an appropriate error message.
Given a user is on the registration form, When they attempt to submit the form with a missing role, Then the system must prevent registration and display an appropriate error message.
Given a user is on the registration form, When they attempt to submit the form with a missing company name, Then the system must prevent registration and display an appropriate error message.
Given a user has successfully logged in, When their session has been inactive for 24 hours, Then their session token must expire.
Given a user's session token has expired, When they attempt to perform an authenticated action, Then the system must require them to re-authenticate.
Given a user is successfully logged in, When they navigate to the Project Dashboard, Then the dashboard must display active user metrics.
Given a user is successfully logged in, When they navigate to the Project Dashboard, Then the dashboard must display system status information.
Given a user is successfully logged in, When they navigate to the Project Dashboard, Then the dashboard must display a list of projects.
test_scenarios: Scenario: Successful user registration via email and password with all required fields (email, full name, role, company name).
Scenario: Successful user login with valid email and password.
Scenario: Successful user registration via Google Sign-in.
Scenario: Attempt to register with an email address that is already associated with an existing account.
Scenario: Attempt to log in with an incorrect password for a valid user.
Scenario: Attempt to log in with an email address that does not exist in the system.
Scenario: Verify that the user is logged out or prompted for re-authentication after 24 hours of inactivity.
Scenario: Verify that the Project Dashboard loads correctly for an authenticated user.
Scenario: Verify that the 'Active User Metrics' section is visible and contains data on the dashboard.
Scenario: Verify that the 'System Status' section is visible and contains data on the dashboard.
Scenario: Verify that the 'Project Lists' section is visible and contains data on the dashboard.
edge_cases: Attempt user registration and login with maximum allowed length inputs for text fields (full name, role, company name) to check for truncation or UI overflow.
Attempt user registration with invalid email formats (e.g., missing @, missing domain, special characters in username).
Attempt concurrent logins for the same user account from different devices or browsers.
Test behavior when the Firebase Authentication service is temporarily unavailable during registration or login.
Test session expiration behavior while an authenticated user is actively performing a long-running operation.
Attempt to access authenticated dashboard features immediately after session expiration.
unresolved_behaviors: What specific criteria define 'super fast' system performance (e.g., specific response times for API calls, page load times, concurrent user capacity benchmarks)? (D005)
What specific data points or calculations are expected for 'active user metrics' on the dashboard (e.g., daily active users, peak concurrent users, average session duration)? (D023)
What specific information should be displayed as 'system status' on the dashboard (e.g., uptime, API health, database connection status, error logs summary, latency)? (D023)

What specific details should be included for each item in the 'project lists' on the dashboard (e.g., project name, creation date, status, owner, last modified, links to project details)? Are there filtering, sorting, or search capabilities expected? (D023, D024, D028)

How will QA verify the strict technical constraint that the PostgreSQL database instance is placed in a private VPC and connected via Serverless VPC Access, preventing exposure to the public internet? (D020)
What criteria will be used to determine if standard PostgreSQL sessions become a 'performance blocker' necessitating a move to Redis for session management? (D010)

decision_history: The main goal for V1 of the Phoenix Project is to deliver a working MVP by September 1st. This is a hard deadline due to the marketing campaign launch scheduled for September 5th.
The V1 MVP must include user authentication and a working project dashboard.
The system needs to be 'super fast' as a general performance requirement.
The technology stack decisions are: React for the frontend, Python (FastAPI) for the backend service, and PostgreSQL for the database using a standard relational model.
For session management, standard PostgreSQL database sessions will be used for V1 to keep it simple and save budget. The use of Redis is deferred to V2 unless it becomes a performance blocker.

The team will use an external authentication provider; Firebase Authentication will be integrated for V1 due to its speed, ease of social logins, and matching pricing requirements. It must be configured to allow Google sign-in and email logins.
The required fields for user registration are email, full name, role, and company name (the latter added for B2B purposes).
Deployment platforms decided: Cloud Run for the FastAPI backend application and Vercel for the React frontend application.
As a strict technical security constraint, the PostgreSQL database instance must be placed in a private VPC and connected via Serverless VPC Access to prevent exposure to the public internet.
The authentication flow needs to be tested thoroughly.
Session token expiration will initially be set to 24 hours.
The project dashboard must display active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Further detailing of dashboard features will be handled in the next synchronization meeting.
Mike has been assigned the task of handling Firebase provisioning and is expected to have the configuration keys ready by July 8th.
A synchronization meeting with the marketing team on the dashboard design is planned for next Wednesday.
dependencies: Marketing campaign launch (September 5th): The V1 MVP's hard deadline of September 1st is directly tied to this external marketing event.
Firebase provisioning (Mike): Mike must provide the Firebase configuration keys by July 8th for authentication development to proceed.
Marketing team: A synchronization meeting is required next Wednesday to align on dashboard design details (D028).

Cloud Run & Serverless VPC Access configuration: The backend deployment on Cloud Run relies on a properly configured Serverless VPC Access to securely connect to the private PostgreSQL database (inferred from D020).
risks: **Timeline Risk: Hard Deadline Constraint (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)** - The V1 MVP has a hard deadline of September 1st, dictated by a marketing campaign launch on September 5th. Any delays in development or unforeseen technical challenges could directly impact the marketing campaign and the overall project's strategic objectives.

**Requirement Clarity Risk: Vague Performance Expectation (Likelihood: High, Impact: Medium)** - The requirement for the system to be 'super fast' (D005) lacks specific, measurable criteria. This ambiguity could lead to misinterpretations, scope creep if continuous optimization is pursued without clear targets, or stakeholder dissatisfaction if performance expectations are not met to an unquantified standard.

**Performance & Rework Risk: Session Management Choice (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium)** - The decision to use standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 (D011) and defer Redis to V2 'unless it's a performance blocker' (D010) introduces a risk. If standard sessions prove insufficient for the 'super fast' requirement (D005), it could necessitate rework and a rushed integration of Redis, impacting V1 stability or delaying V2 features.

**Dependency Risk: Firebase Provisioning (Likelihood: Low, Impact: High)** - The development of user authentication relies on Mike providing Firebase configuration keys by July 8th (D027). A delay in this dependency could block a critical path item for V1.

**Scope Risk: Undefined Dashboard Features (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: Medium)** - While core metrics for the dashboard are identified, further detailing is deferred to the next sync meeting (D024). This delay in defining full scope could lead to late requirements, rework, or a reduced feature set if not managed diligently against the tight V1 deadline.
alignment_gaps: **Performance Definition:** The requirement for the system to be 'super fast' (D005) is subjective and lacks concrete metrics. This could lead to differing expectations among stakeholders regarding what constitutes acceptable performance.

**Dashboard Feature Scope:** While essential metrics are identified, the full detailing of dashboard features has been explicitly deferred to a future meeting (D024). This indicates that the complete scope and functionality are not yet fully aligned among all stakeholders, risking potential disagreements or changes as the deadline approaches.

stakeholder.business_summary: The team is focused on delivering the Phoenix Project's first version (V1) by September 1st, a hard deadline driven by an upcoming marketing campaign. This initial release will include essential user authentication, a project dashboard, and is designed for fast performance and secure operations. Key technology decisions have been made to streamline development and meet these critical business requirements.
key_impact: The product launch is firmly set for early September to align with a major marketing campaign, ensuring immediate market presence.
Users will experience a 'super fast' system with easy sign-up options via Google or email, including essential B2B registration fields like company name.
Critical user data and system integrity will be maintained through strong security measures, including an external authentication provider and a protected database.
The core features of user login and a project dashboard will be available from day one, providing immediate value to users.

issues.description: The Stakeholder view's 'decisions_needing_alignment' and 'major_unresolved_questions' are empty, which contradicts the 'alignment_gaps' identified in the PM view for significant items like 'Performance Definition' (D005) and 'Dashboard Feature Scope' (D024). This understates the current level of clarity required for these critical areas for the stakeholder audience.
issues.suggestion: Update the Stakeholder view to reflect that performance metrics require definition and dashboard features are still being detailed, aligning with the PM view's 'alignment_gaps'. For example, list 'Defining specific performance metrics' and 'Finalizing detailed dashboard features' under 'decisions_needing_alignment' or 'major_unresolved_questions', possibly with a high-level summary.

review.reviewer_notes: The overall quality of the generated outputs is high, with excellent traceability to the extracted decisions and comprehensive details provided for each role. No critical hallucinations or missing sources were identified. A minor inconsistency in the Stakeholder view regarding unresolved questions/alignment gaps was noted (warning severity), which should be addressed for a more complete and consistent report across all audiences.
